subject: [PATCH 16/19] nfsd: validate sockaddr length per family in listener_set

2026-06-09.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the NFS daemon's netlink listener set functionality by validating the length of sockaddr structures per address family. The issue arose from an overly permissive policy that allowed a 16-byte NFSD_A_SOCK_ADDR with sa_family=AF_INET6, causing an out-of-bounds read across multiple consumers. To fix this, the patch tightens the policy to require at least 16 bytes for NFSD_A_SOCK_ADDR and adds per-family length validation in both nlmsg_for_each_attr_type loops.
